old_claim.note: Internet of Things (IoT) & Ubiquitous Connectivity provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Wireless connected devices, portals, and apps let RPM scale from local measurement into continuous remote clinical workflows.

source_shape.source_locator: RPM review definition and related discussion of wearable devices, web portals, mobile apps, and transmitted data.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The review describes RPM as wearable-device data automatically transmitted to portals or mobile apps for self-monitoring and clinical assessment.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports a scaling edge because connected-device infrastructure turns local ambulatory measurements into remote monitoring workflows at care-system scale.

ontology_before: The IoT edge was a generic enabling claim.
ontology_after: IoT/connectivity is a scaling and workflow dependency for RPM deployment rather than the physiological measurement method itself.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: the IoT edge is typed as commercial_or_scaling_dependency.
would_reject_if: The node were scoped to local ambulatory monitoring with no remote data flow.
A source showed remote patient monitoring at scale does not use connected-device data transmission.
The edge were made required and over-modeled IoT as the only possible route for RPM.
short_reason: Connected-device infrastructure scales RPM workflows beyond local measurement.
